title: Honda Smart Key System Structure Description - Control/Function

blocks.text: Honda Smart Key System Structure Description - Control/Function
Mutual authentication is performed between the MICU and the Honda Smart Key using the lock/unlock switch input, and the door lock and door unlock signals and the answer-back buzzer signal are output.
The keyless transmitter signal from the Honda Smart Key is received by the RF unit built into the MICU, and the MICU matches the signal and outputs a door lock/unlock signal.

If key authentication between the Honda Smart Key and the MICU is not possible due to a system malfunction or low battery voltage in the Honda Smart Key, press the ENGINE START STOP switch once, and while the ENGINE START STOP switch indicator is blinking, move the ENGINE START STOP switch closer to the MICU. The immobilizer receiver built into the ENGINE START STOP switch receives the immobilizer signal and sends the immobilizer signal to the MICU to deactivate the immobilizer function. The immobilizer function is deactivated by sending an immobilizer signal to the MICU.
The status of the Honda smart key system is displayed on the combination meter via B-CAN communication.

When a door is opened or closed, or a door lock is operated, the in-vehicle LF antenna searches for the presence of the Honda Smart Key in the vehicle. When the door lock knob switch is turned on (locked state) and the door switch or tailgate latch switch is input in that order and the Honda Smart Key is detected inside the vehicle, the MICU sends a door unlock signal to prevent the key from being locked.

After the MICU receives the ENGINE START STOP switch input and authenticates the Honda Smart Key, the MICU supplies power to the electronic steering lock and checks if the electronic steering lock is registered. If the registered electronic steering lock is locked, the MICU sends a lock/unlock signal. After the electronic steering lock is unlocked, it feeds back to the MICU to enable the shift to ACC/ON mode. If the electronic steering lock is not unlocked, the MICU will not enter ACC/ON mode. In case of abnormality, the MICU sends an indicator signal to the combination meter via B-CAN.
Up to six Honda Smart Keys can be registered on the Smart ECU's nonvolatile memory (EEPROM) via HDS.

In an emergency while driving, pressing the ENGINE START STOP switch at least twice consecutively or holding it down for 1.5 seconds or longer can put the car into ACC/OFF (LOCK) mode without locking the steering wheel.
